headline: Four Undangs Declared the Ruler Deposed. Three Hold Office. One Does Not.
context: On 19 April 2026, the four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the proposed 12th Ruler. State government rejects it: one Undang was dismissed in May 2025.

cards.big: Four Undangs declared Tuanku Muhriz deposed and named Tunku Nadzaruddin the 12th Ruler. Three hold office; one was dismissed 11 months earlier.
cards.sub: State government rejects the declaration. The same Undang who proclaimed Muhriz in 2008 — since removed for 33 offences — signed this one too.

cards.big: The constitution lets the Undangs collectively call the Ruler to withdraw. Whether three alone — without the dismissed fourth — suffice is untested.
cards.sub: BERNAMA and Malay Mail cite Articles 10-12; The Star cites 10-13. All agree: rejection rests on Mubarak no longer holding office.

cards.big: The same Undang who read the deposal on 19 April 2026 had proclaimed Tuanku Muhriz as 11th Ruler on 29 December 2008.
cards.sub: In 1967, the Undang bypassed Muhriz for the throne at 19, electing his uncle Tuanku Ja'afar. Tunku Nadzaruddin — Ja'afar's son — is now the proposed successor.

cards.big: Mubarak was dismissed 13 May 2025 for 33 offences against Telaga Undang custom. The DKU ratified the dismissal two days before he signed the declaration.
cards.sub: The DKU sat 17 April 2026, presided by Tuanku Muhriz. Ibu Soko Klana Hulu and Anak Waris Klana Hulu declined to give Mubarak a mandate.

cards.big: Undangs cited alleged royal misconduct from a 5 March inquiry. Institutions called the act menderhaka; JAIS and police were notified.
cards.sub: No state, customary, or judicial body has publicly addressed the misconduct claim itself. Daulat was defended before the underlying allegation was examined.

cards.big: The real question is not whether one dismissed Undang can act. It is whether three Undangs alone can invoke a provision designed for four.
A constitution built on the collective authority of four Undangs is being tested by three. No court has ruled, and the institutions have defended the throne first.
